## BftOrderingService


Signed integer types are used, even though they are always non-negative in network-sent data, to match the Java gRPC
  implementation, which uses signed integers because the JVM doesn't support unsigned integers.

  <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-row">

      <span class="x2mdx-ref-type-badge">string</span>
      
    </div>
    
    <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-description">The fingerprint/id of the keypair used to create this signature and needed to verify. If a signature delegation is defined, this fingerprint/id is not used to produce the signature, and, instead is used to sign the delegation.</div>

  <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-row">
    <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-main">
      <code class="x2mdx-ref-field-name">signing_algorithm_spec</code>

  </div>
  
  <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-row">
    <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-main">
      <code class="x2mdx-ref-field-name">signature_delegation</code>
      <span class="x2mdx-ref-type-badge">SignatureDelegation</span>
      
    </div>
    
    <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-description">An optional signature delegation where a long-term signing key authorizes a session signing key to sign on its behalf.</div>
    
  </div>
  
</div>

  
  
</div>

<div class="x2mdx-ref-schema" id="schema-com-digitalasset-canton-crypto-v30-signaturedelegation">
  <div class="x2mdx-ref-schema-head">
    <h3>com.digitalasset.canton.crypto.v30.SignatureDelegation</h3>
    
    <p class="x2mdx-ref-schema-summary">7 fields</p>
    
  </div>
  
  
<div class="x2mdx-ref-fields">
  
  <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-row">
    <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-main">
      <code class="x2mdx-ref-field-name">session_key</code>
      <span class="x2mdx-ref-type-badge">bytes</span>
      
    </div>
    
    <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-description">ASN.1 + DER-encoding of X.509 SubjectPublicKeyInfo structure of the session public key: https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc5280#section-4.1. This key was used to produce the actual signature.</div>
    
  </div>
  
  <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-row">
    <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-main">
      <code class="x2mdx-ref-field-name">session_key_spec</code>
      <span class="x2mdx-ref-type-badge">SigningKeySpec</span>
      
    </div>
    
    <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-description">The signing key spec for the session key.</div>
    
  </div>
  
  <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-row">
    <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-main">
      <code class="x2mdx-ref-field-name">validity_period_from_inclusive</code>
      <span class="x2mdx-ref-type-badge">int64</span>
      
    </div>
    
    <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-description">A timestamp in microseconds of UTC time since Unix epoch that indicates when the session key is considered valid.</div>
    
  </div>
  
  <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-row">
    <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-main">
      <code class="x2mdx-ref-field-name">validity_period_duration_seconds</code>
      <span class="x2mdx-ref-type-badge">uint32</span>
      
    </div>
    
    <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-description">Duration in seconds indicating how long the session key remains valid. The validity period starts from `validity_period_from_inclusive` above.</div>
    
  </div>
  
  <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-row">
    <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-main">
      <code class="x2mdx-ref-field-name">format</code>
      <span class="x2mdx-ref-type-badge">SignatureFormat</span>
      
    </div>
    
    <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-description">The format of the signature</div>
    
  </div>
  
  <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-row">
    <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-main">
      <code class="x2mdx-ref-field-name">signature</code>
      <span class="x2mdx-ref-type-badge">bytes</span>
      
    </div>
    
    <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-description">The signature of the combined hash of the session key fingerprint, validity period, and synchronizer id, by the long-term key. This signature authorizes the session key to act on behalf of the long-term key.</div>
    
  </div>
  
  <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-row">
    <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-main">
      <code class="x2mdx-ref-field-name">signing_algorithm_spec</code>
      <span class="x2mdx-ref-type-badge">SigningAlgorithmSpec</span>
      
    </div>
    
    <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-description">The signing algorithm specification used to produce this signature</div>
    
  </div>
  
</div>

  
  
</div>

  <div class="x2mdx-ref-schema-head">
    <h3>com.digitalasset.canton.synchronizer.sequencing.sequencer.bftordering.v30.BlockTransferRequest</h3>
    
    <p class="x2mdx-ref-schema-summary">1 fields</p>
    
  </div>
  
  
<div class="x2mdx-ref-fields">
  
  <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-row">
    <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-main">
      <code class="x2mdx-ref-field-name">epoch</code>
      <span class="x2mdx-ref-type-badge">int64</span>
      
    </div>
    
  </div>

</div>

<div class="x2mdx-ref-schema" id="schema-com-digitalasset-canton-synchronizer-sequencing-sequencer-bftordering-v30-blocktransferresponse">
  <div class="x2mdx-ref-schema-head">
    <h3>com.digitalasset.canton.synchronizer.sequencing.sequencer.bftordering.v30.BlockTransferResponse</h3>
    
    <p class="x2mdx-ref-schema-summary">1 fields</p>
    
  </div>
  
  <p class="x2mdx-ref-schema-description">A thin wrapper for a commit certificate. As long as it merely contains a commit certificate (that in turn includes signed and verified data), its signature verification can be safely skipped. As a result, any node can help with state transfer (even when sending responses signed with a new/rotated key).</p>
  
  
<div class="x2mdx-ref-fields">
  
  <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-row">
    <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-main">
      <code class="x2mdx-ref-field-name">commit_certificate</code>
      <span class="x2mdx-ref-type-badge">CommitCertificate</span>
      
    </div>
    
    <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-description">Avoid adding more data that would require signing to allow skipping the outer message&#x27;s signature verification!</div>
    
  </div>
  
</div>

  
  
</div>

<div class="x2mdx-ref-schema" id="schema-com-digitalasset-canton-synchronizer-sequencing-sequencer-bftordering-v30-canonicalcommitset">
  <div class="x2mdx-ref-schema-head">
    <h3>com.digitalasset.canton.synchronizer.sequencing.sequencer.bftordering.v30.CanonicalCommitSet</h3>
    
    <p class="x2mdx-ref-schema-summary">1 fields</p>
    
  </div>
  
  <p class="x2mdx-ref-schema-description">A canonical (fixed) set of (usually 2f+1) Commit messages that completed the recent stable block. Check the BFT time design for more details.</p>
  
  
<div class="x2mdx-ref-fields">
  
  <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-row">
    <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-main">
      <code class="x2mdx-ref-field-name">canonical_commits</code>
      <span class="x2mdx-ref-type-badge">repeated SignedMessage</span>
      
    </div>
    
  </div>
  
</div>

  
  
</div>

<div class="x2mdx-ref-schema" id="schema-com-digitalasset-canton-synchronizer-sequencing-sequencer-bftordering-v30-commit">
  <div class="x2mdx-ref-schema-head">
    <h3>com.digitalasset.canton.synchronizer.sequencing.sequencer.bftordering.v30.Commit</h3>
    
    <p class="x2mdx-ref-schema-summary">2 fields</p>
    
  </div>
  
  <p class="x2mdx-ref-schema-description">A `Commit` means that a node have received a quorum of `Prepare` Upon receiving a quorum of `Commit`, a node considers the round complete</p>
  
  
<div class="x2mdx-ref-fields">
  
  <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-row">
    <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-main">
      <code class="x2mdx-ref-field-name">block_hash</code>
      <span class="x2mdx-ref-type-badge">bytes</span>
      
    </div>
    
  </div>
  
  <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-row">
    <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-main">
      <code class="x2mdx-ref-field-name">local_timestamp</code>
      <span class="x2mdx-ref-type-badge">int64</span>
      
    </div>
    
    <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-description">creation time, in microseconds of UTC time since Unix epoch</div>
    
  </div>
  
</div>

  
  
</div>

  </div>

    <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-main">

  </div>

  <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-row">

    </div>
    
  </div>
  
</div>

  
  
</div>

<div class="x2mdx-ref-schema" id="schema-com-digitalasset-canton-synchronizer-sequencing-sequencer-bftordering-v30-epochstatus">
  <div class="x2mdx-ref-schema-head">

    <p class="x2mdx-ref-schema-summary">2 fields</p>
    
  </div>
  
  <p class="x2mdx-ref-schema-description">Node&#x27;s status of consensus for the whole epoch. Acts as a retransmission request where receiving node will compute what they can retransmit based on what the requesting node is lacking from looking at this status message.</p>
  
  
<div class="x2mdx-ref-fields">
  
  <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-row">
    <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-main">

    </div>
    
  </div>
  
  <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-row">
    <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-main">
      <code class="x2mdx-ref-field-name">segments</code>
      <span class="x2mdx-ref-type-badge">repeated SegmentStatus</span>
      
    </div>
    
  </div>
  
</div>

  
  
</div>

  </div>
